channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What to know about activist Mahmoud Khalil and his attorneys' plan to appeal his deportation ruling
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — An immigration judge has ruled that a Palestinian Columbia University graduate student who participated in protests against Israel can be deported. Mahmoud Khalil's attorneys said they will appeal Friday's ruling.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mideast mediator Oman at the center of a key first Iran-US meeting over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: MUSCAT, Oman (AP) — Once again, some of the highest stakes in Middle East geopolitics will be discussed in this quiet coastal city without skyscrapers.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump admin adds migrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The request from Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem to place the migrants on the “Ineligible Master File” maintained by the SSA would bar migrants from receiving benefits or being able to work in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Strict Idaho abortion ban loosened by judge's ruling on medical exemptions
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An abortion in Idaho is not prohibited if pregnancy complications could cause a woman's death, even if that death “is neither imminent nor assured,” a state judge said Friday in a ruling that loosens one of the strictest abortion bans in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Trump administration offers no details on mistakenly deported man after court ruling
item.description: A federal judge on Friday lambasted a government lawyer who couldn’t explain what, if anything, President Donald Trump’s administration has done to arrange for the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month to a notorious prison in El Salvador. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jewish families channel Passover story after devastating losses in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: PASADENA, California (AP) — Aty Rotter lost her family home and her spiritual home in the ravenous fire that scorched a large swath of Los Angeles County earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ghislaine Maxwell, Epstein accomplice, appeals case to Supreme Court
item.description: Maxwell was convicted back in December 2021 for helping Jeffrey Epstein sexually exploit and abuse underage girls.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Gas explosion causes $3 million in damages at Spanish Fork dental office
item.description: A gas explosion caused an estimated $3 million in damages at a Spanish Fork dental office Friday morning, according to Spanish Fork Fire & EMS.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Florida universities to deputize campus police for immigration enforcement
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The police departments of at least three public universities in Florida are seeking agreements with the federal government to carry out immigration enforcement on campus.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Universal announces UK theme park amid 'epic' growth of parks business
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences is expanding its global theme park footprint, confirming a new theme park will be built in the U.K. -- the latest in a trio of ambitious projects for the company.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Greenland space base commander removed after Vance criticism
item.description: The Trump administration removed the Greenland space base commander Thursday after discovering the leader sent out an email breaking stride with the vice president.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Family from Spain, pilot killed in NYC sightseeing helicopter crash
item.description: Photos posted on the helicopter company’s website showed the couple and their children smiling as they boarded just before the flight took off.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, WGN 9

item.title: No, Casey Anthony did not open a Branson daycare
item.description: Google Trends showed a variety of topics being searched in Missouri on Thursday morning---sports, weather, the lottery, and one specific question: Did Casey Anthony open a daycare in Branson, Missouri?
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, KRON4

item.title: Judge will halt Trump administration from ending humanitarian parole for people from four countries
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge said Thursday that she will prevent the Trump administration from ordering hundreds of thousands of Cubans, Haitians, Nicaraguans and Venezuelans with temporary legal status to leave the country later this month.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US government moves for release of ex-FBI informant who fabricated bribery story about the Bidens
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The U.S. government now wants a former FBI informant who fabricated a story about former President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepting bribes to be released from prison while he appeals his conviction, it was revealed Thursday in court records.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Utah labor leaders in final push to repeal controversial collective bargaining ban
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) -- Labor leaders are racing against the clock as they now have less than a week to get enough signatures to place a referendum on the ballot to overturn one of this year’s most controversial bills – H.B. 267.
item.dc:creator: Yeonseung Kim, ABC 4

item.title: Movie theaters erupt in chaos as part of 'Minecraft' social media trend
item.description: Rowdy crowds of young people have been disrupting screenings of the newly released "A Minecraft Movie.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Report: Drug lord linked to DEA agent’s death freed
item.description: One of the three leaders of the Guadalajara cartel linked to the torture-slaying of Drug Enforcement Administration Agent Enrique “Kiki” Camarena in 1985 has been released in Mexico, according to news reports.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: US egg prices increase to record high, dashing hopes of cheap eggs by Easter
item.description: U.S. egg prices increased again last month to reach a new record-high of $6.23 per dozen despite President Donald Trump’s predictions, a drop in wholesale prices and no egg farms having bird flu outbreaks.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized state party in swing state is leaving
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized the party in the presidential swing state announced Thursday he will be leaving his position in June after a six-year run that included a string of victories and “agonizingly close” losses.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mourners pay tribute to merengue icon Rubby Pérez, who died in the Dominican roof collapse
item.description: Fans and countrymen on Thursday shared their remembrances of Dominican Republic music icon Rubby Pérez, who was among the scores of people killed this week after the roof at the Jet Set club in Santo Domingo collapsed. He was 69.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 97-year-old woman dies after daughter intentionally left her out in the cold: report
item.description: The death of Corrye Brewer, a 97-year-old woman who died of hypothermia in January, has been ruled as a homicide by the El Paso County Coroner's Office, according to the autopsy report.
item.dc:creator: Dara Korn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers say airplanes bumped each other on runway at Reagan National
item.description: Multiple lawmakers on Thursday said their airplane was bumped by another plane while waiting on the runway at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Hearing looks at rules for evidence and other details in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Prosecutors and attorneys for a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students in 2022 argued some of the final ground rules they want for Bryan Kohberger's trial in a hearing Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida brings charges against a man jailed on federal counts of trying to assassinate Trump
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — A man already jailed on federal charges of attempting to assassinate then-presidential candidate Donald Trump as he golfed last fall will face additional state terrorism and attempted murder charges, Florida's attorney general said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House Republicans pass bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
item.description: House Republicans for a second time passed a stand-alone bill that would require proof of citizenship to vote in federal elections and impose voter roll purge requirements on states. The legislation — formally titled the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act — passed in a 220-208 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Brawl erupts between college baseball teams
item.description: A fight broke out Tuesday night during a game between the Spring Hill College and University of Mobile baseball teams.
item.dc:creator: Summer Poole, WFLA, NBC

item.title: UTA announces 'significant' changes to several bus services including new routes
item.description: The Utah Transit Authority said significant changes are coming to its services in Davis, Salt Lake, and Utah County.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: S&P 500 sinks 3.5% as Wall Street swings from euphoria back to worries about US-China trade war
item.description: The losses for U.S. stocks accelerated Thursday after the White House clarified that Chinese imports will be tariffed at 145%, not the 125% rate that Trump had written about in his posting on Truth Social Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Author Jillian Lauren, wife of Weezer bassist, arrested after being shot by police
item.description: Jillian Lauren, author and wife of Weezer bassist Scott Shriner, was shot and injured by Los Angeles police and arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after pointing a gun at officers from her front yard, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Russian-American ballerina from Los Angeles released from Russia
item.description: A U.S.-Russian dual national imprisoned in Russia on treason charges was freed Thursday in exchange for a Russian man jailed on smuggling charges in the U.S., a prisoner swap that comes as Moscow and Washington have made efforts to repair ties.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Shark attack survivor presses state for alert system to keep people safe in the water
item.description: An Alabama teen who narrowly survived a fierce shark attack last year said she hopes a proposed alert system before state lawmakers can help keep others safe in the water.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hope of finding survivors fades in aftermath of Dominican club roof collapse
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — Rescue crews in the Dominican Republic on Thursday dug through the remains of a legendary nightclub whose roof collapsed earlier this week, killing at least 184 people, but hope of finding survivors was slim.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sweepstakes giant Publishers Clearing House files for bankruptcy
item.description: Publishers Clearing House, famous for its big checks and sweepstakes, files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy as it shifts to digital advertising.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Local businesses react to Trump's tariffs, prepare for potential long-term effects
item.description: Local businesses are feeling anxiety and uncertainty around President Donald Trump’s tariffs — despite the president backing down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days today.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan May, ABC 4

item.title: Lottery player claims he was cheated out of $95 million jackpot win
item.description: A Texas lottery player filed a lawsuit in Travis County on Tuesday alleging his May 2023 Lotto Texas jackpot prize was severely diminished by $95 million because a group of lottery retailers and a London-based sports gambling company conspired to rig a lottery drawing less than a month before his wi
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Americans convicted in Congo of a botched coup attempt now face US charges
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Three Americans repatriated to the United States from Congo this week have been charged with participating in an elaborate coup attempt aimed at overthrowing the African nation’s government last year, the U.S. Justice Department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4
